name: linux_audit_logs
explanation: Linux Audit Logs is a logs-only integration. The spec contains a single `logs` template tailing `/var/log/audit/audit.log` (source/service `linux-audit-logs`); there is no Agent metric check (no check.py — `datadog_checks/linux_audit_logs/` only has `__about__.py` and `__init__.py`). There is no host/port/URL/credential to discover and no per-instance options that drive endpoint resolution. Auto-config in the network-probe sense does not apply: the only "discovery" needed is whether the Agent host has auditd running and writing to the standard audit log path, which is a host-local presence check tied to a fixed file path rather than a probe. Treat as impossible like other logs-only / instrumentation-only integrations (pattern N).
notes: Logs-only integration (pattern N analogue). No Agent check class is dispatched, only a logs tailer. Hint from prompt confirms: classified as impossible.
